# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_gtm_dns_listener (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `address` (String) Specifies the IP address on which the system listens.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Specifies the name of the DNS Listener.

### Optional

- `advertise` (Boolean) Specifies whether this Listener's address is advertised to surrounding routers.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_lasthop` (String) Specifies whether to automatically map the last hop for pools or not.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Provides a brief description for DNS Listener.
- `disabled_vlans` (String) List of VLANs to be disabled. If the partition is not specified in the VLAN, then the `partition` option of this module will be used. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the `enabled_vlans` parameters.
- `enabled_vlans` (String) List of VLANs to be enabled. When a VLAN named `all` is used, all VLANs will be allowed. VLANs can be specified with or without the leading partition. If the partition is not specified in the VLAN, then the `partition` option of this module will be used. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the `disabled_vlans` parameter.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fallback_persistence` (String) Specifies a fallback persistence profile for the Listener to use when the default persistence profile is not available.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ip_protocol` (String) Specifies the protocol on which this Listener receives network traffic.
- `irules` (String) Specifies list of iRules to run on the Listener. iRules help automate the intercepting, processing, and routing of application traffic. If you want to remove existing iRules, provide an empty list value; `[]`. See the documentation for an example.
- `last_hop_pool` (String) Specifies the name of the last hop pool that you want the Listener to use to direct reply traffic to the last hop router.
- `mask` (String) Specifies the netmask for a network Listener only. Netmask clarifies whether the host bit is an actual zero or a wildcard representation.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `pool` (String) Specifies a default pool to which the Listener automatically directs traffic.
- `port` (Number) Specifies the port on which the Listener listens for connections. Valid range of values is between `0` and `65535` inclusive.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `source_port` (String) Specifies whether the system preserves the source port of the connection.
- `state` (String) DNS Listener state. When `present`, ensures the pool is created and enabled. When `absent`, ensures the pool is removed from the system. When `enabled` or `disabled`, ensures the pool is enabled or disabled respectively) on the remote device.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `translate_address` (Boolean) Enables or disables address translation for the Listener.
- `translate_port` (Boolean) Enables or disables port translation.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `enabled` (Boolean) Provides DNS Listener state.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
